author: Dr. Emily Brown
hpi: Post-discharge telephone follow-up with Mr. Morrison, 72-year-old male discharged yesterday after observation admission for abdominal discomfort. CT abdomen showed hepatic steatosis, renal cysts, diverticulosis, and umbilical hernia — all chronic/incidental findings. He was discharged on temporarily increased furosemide (60mg BID x 5 days for mild volume overload). Patient reports feeling better at home. Abdominal discomfort has mostly resolved, rated 1/10 intermittently. He tolerated breakfast and lunch without difficulty. He has been checking his weight — 98.3 kg this morning (down 0.5 kg from discharge, suggesting response to increased diuresis). He voided well overnight with nocturia x2. No swelling in legs that he can see. No chest pain, worsening shortness of breath, or fever. He has been compliant with medications. Metformin remains held pending Cr recheck tomorrow. His wife confirms he is eating a low-sodium diet and drinking approximately 1.5 liters of fluid. She reports his mood seems slightly better with being home.

reviewOfSystems.Constitutional: Fatigue improved. No fever or chills.
reviewOfSystems.Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or worsening dyspnea. No orthopnea.
reviewOfSystems.GI: Abdominal discomfort nearly resolved (1/10). Good appetite. Normal bowel movement. No nausea, vomiting, melena, or hematochezia.
reviewOfSystems.GU: Nocturia x2. No dysuria. Good urine output.

physicalExam.General: Telephone encounter — physical exam not performed. Patient reports feeling well, voice sounds clear and energetic.
physicalExam.Cardiovascular: Not assessed (telephone). Patient denies chest pain, palpitations, or worsening edema.
physicalExam.Respiratory: Not assessed (telephone). Patient denies cough or worsening dyspnea.
physicalExam.Abdomen: Not assessed (telephone). Patient reports significant improvement in abdominal discomfort.
physicalExam.Extremities: Not assessed (telephone). Patient denies worsening swelling.
physicalExam.Neurologic: Not assessed (telephone). Patient reports stable neuropathy symptoms.

assessment.notes: Symptoms nearly resolved at home. CT showed no acute pathology. Most likely related to mild hepatic congestion from HF volume overload and/or hepatic steatosis. Responding to increased diuresis.

assessment.diagnosis: Heart Failure with Reduced EF — responding to increased diuresis
assessment.notes: Weight down 0.5 kg from discharge. Symptom improvement. Home vitals stable. Tolerating increased furosemide (60mg BID x 5 days).
Metformin held. Cr recheck scheduled for tomorrow. No symptoms of contrast nephropathy (urine output adequate, no new edema).

plan.action: Resolving. Continue monitoring at home. If pain returns or worsens, or if new symptoms develop (fever, melena, severe pain), return to ED immediately.

Continue furosemide 60mg BID through Sunday (5-day course). Then return to baseline 60mg AM/40mg PM on Monday. Continue daily weights. If weight drops below 97 kg, reduce to baseline dosing earlier. Call if weight increases >3 lbs in a day.
Cr recheck tomorrow at outpatient lab. If Cr returns to baseline (~1.96), resume metformin 500mg BID. Results will be reviewed by PCP office.
PCP appointment confirmed for 9/18. Lab draw tomorrow AM. Will call patient with Cr results. Cardiology TEE scheduling in progress.

attestation:
Telephone follow-up encounter documented. Patient assessed by phone. Plan reviewed and agreed upon by patient and wife.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Emily Brown, MD
Date: 9/13/2024
